channel.title: BBC News
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Stormont parties demand 'full transparency' over DUP safeguarding
item.description: The Democratic Unionist Party says it is in the process of commissioning an independent review into safeguarding issues.

item.title: Resident doctors take strike action over pay
item.description: The BMA said there will "undoubtedly be cancellation of operations, outpatient appointments and elective care" but "patient safety will still be prioritised".

item.title: Motorcyclist dies following two-vehicle collision
item.description: Police say a 55-year-old man who was from the Bellaghy area died at the scene.

item.title: 'Frankenchickens': The debate about welfare and sustainability
item.description: With protein goals pushing demand for poultry to an all time high, how much do we really think about how chickens are reared?

item.title: New scheme announced to tackle obesity in Northern Ireland
item.description: The Regional Obesity Management Service (ROMS) is expected to begin rolling out in early autumn 2026.

item.title: Man critical after suspected hit-and-run
item.description: Police are appealing for information about a suspected hit-and-run incident in Dromore after a man was hit by a car.

item.title: Large blaze at former convent building 'deliberate'
item.description: The Fire Service say they are scaling back their response, but they plan to remain at the location for a number of hours.

item.title: Arrest made as police attend scene of suspicious death
item.description: Local politicians have expressed their sadness at the death of a person in the Helen's Wood Court area of west Belfast

item.title: Woman taken to hospital as fire destroys salon
item.description: Five of the occupants self-evacuated and one woman had to be rescued, before she was taken to hospital for smoke inhalation.

item.title: Bannigan rues lack of chances after All-Ireland exit
item.description: Monaghan manager Gabriel Bannigan says his side not being able to take advantage of the extra player during their quarter-final exit by Louth "makes it all the more devastating".

item.title: Barrister strike 'caused additional trauma' to victims as 2,500 cases delayed
item.description: Nearly 2,500 cases were impacted by the six-month-long barristers' strike.

item.title: Was historic weekend the biggest in Irish cricket history?
item.description: After the men's and women's sides both made history with victories over India and the West Indies, was it the biggest weekend in Irish cricket history?

item.title: Has Ireland lost a friend with the departure of Sir Keir Starmer?
item.description: Sir Keir Starmer announced his resignation earlier this week, what was his relationship like with the Republic of Ireland?

item.title: Crusaders placed under transfer embargo by IFA
item.description: Crusaders have been placed under a transfer embargo by the Irish Football Association [IFA] after a breach of the Northern Ireland Football League [NIFL] rules and regulations.

item.title: George Best's European Cup medal sells for £300k
item.description: The mazy winger was presented with his medal after Manchester United beat Benfica at Wembley Stadium.

item.title: 'Unbelievable' - Hunter savours first World Cup win
item.description: Amy Hunter praises the "absolutely outrageous" Orla Prendergast after they combine to inspire Ireland to a long-awaited first Women's T20 World Cup win.

item.title: Kerry loss 'will take a while to get over' - Kilpatrick
item.description: Tyrone midfielder Conn Kilpatrick says the 2-25 to 0-27 All-Ireland SFC quarter-final defeat by Kerry will "take a while to get over".

item.title: Rider airlifted to hospital after Tandragee crash
item.description: A rider is airlifted to hospital after crashing during the Senior Support race at the Tandragee 100.

item.title: Man dies following single-car crash
item.description: Emergency services attended but the driver, 31-year-old Matthew McIlroy, was pronounced dead at the scene.

item.title: Staff concerned hospital arrangements 'not safe', says watchdog
item.description: The Regulation and Quality Improvement Authority carried out an unannounced inspection at the South West Acute Hospital between November 2025 and February 2026.

item.title: O'Rourke frustrated by 'really, really harsh calls'
item.description: Tyrone manager Malachy O'Rourke feels some of the calls against his side in their 2-25 to 0-27 All-Ireland SFC quarter-final defeat to Kerry were "really, really harsh."

item.title: Special Olympics athletes 'so proud' to represent Team Ulster
item.description: Almost 100 athletes were welcomed home with a special event at Stormont on Thursday.

item.title: Auction house criticised over sale of 3,000-year-old mummy head
item.description: Academics question the ethics of a UK auction house selling human heads that are thousands of years old.

item.title: PSNI officer 'had intelligence on Donaldson abuse a year before complaint'
item.description: A BBC investigation has been told a detective and a child safeguarding expert identified Donaldson after speaking to a victim.

item.title: DUP commissioning review following Donaldson conviction
item.description: The DUP leadership said they are deeply concerned by allegations that have surfaced in recent days.

item.title: A gay sauna visit and an 'exploited' young woman – the double life of Jeffrey Donaldson
item.description: BBC NI Spotlight investigates the long-serving Christian politician jailed for child sex offences.

item.title: Donaldson to give up knighthood and seat on Privy Council
item.description: The moves were confirmed in a statement issued by his solicitor John McBurney.

item.title: Donaldson's downfall shakes NI's battle-hardened political landscape
item.description: Donaldson has been told to expect a "lengthy sentence" for a string of child sex crimes, including rape.

item.title: Jeffrey Donaldson's life in politics: From DUP leader to convicted child sex abuser
item.description: Jeffrey Donaldson's career took him from the UUP to the DUP leadership, but he now faces a prison sentence after being convicted of child sex abuse.

item.title: How four weeks in court unravelled 'sinful and deceitful' Jeffrey Donaldson
item.description: The former DUP leader entered calm court on day one calm and relaxed, but by the end he was alone as the jury found him guilty on 18 child sex abuse charges.

item.title: Eleanor Donaldson: What is a trial of the facts?
item.description: Sir Jeffrey Donaldson's wife Eleanor was a co-accused in his child sex abuse trial - but could not be found guilty as she faced a trial of the facts.

item.title: Ex DUP leader Donaldson guilty of child sex abuse charges including one of rape
item.description: The former MP is remanded in custody while he awaits sentencing for 18 offences.

item.title: Could legal 'personhood' help save Lough Neagh?
item.description: Rights of Nature could give Lough Neagh the same status as a person, entitled to the same protections.

item.title: Battles, weapons and jewellery making: Bringing the Vikings to life
item.description: It's the fourth year in a row the festival has been held at Ballydugan Medieval Settlement.

item.title: After Muckamore dust settles, what should care look like?
item.description: The final report from the public inquiry into abuse at the hospital was published last week.

item.title: How has the Iran war hit the NI economy?
item.description: The rapid fall in the price of oil in the last two weeks means there are hopes that the economic impact could be short-lived.

item.title: India-born Moondra realises dream in famous Ireland win
item.description: India-born bowler Jai Moondra says helping Ireland claim a historic T20 win over the world champions is a "dream come true".

item.title: 'Young people with disabilities shouldn't have to change for society,' say youth workers
item.description: Youth workers from New Lodge in north Belfast and pupils from Harberton Special School designed the #hearMeOut campaign.

item.title: Skies lit up by 'epic' lightning show of 9,000 strikes
item.description: Almost 9,000 lightning strikes were recorded on Thursday night - mostly across Counties Londonderry and Donegal.

item.title: How has Northern Ireland's economy fared since Brexit?
item.description: A decade on from Brexit, BBC News NI's John Campbell looks at whether the region has benefited from having dual access to the EU and GB markets.

item.title: From saying 'I do' to the first dance at a Teddy Swims concert
item.description: After forgetting they had tickets for Teddy Swims in Belfast, the Bangor couple booked their wedding day for the same date.

item.title: BBC Newsline signed summary
item.description: A signed summary of BBC Newsline.

item.title: Princess Royal visits stables during Northern Ireland visit
item.description: The princess, who is Vice Patron of The British Horse Society, was shown around stables in Katesbridge, County Down.

item.title: Heatwave brings challenges for vulnerable people
item.description: Brian Shields used to "love the heat", but living with MS means the current heatwave can affect his muscles making it difficult to move.

item.title: Tyrone 'can beat Kerry' - Logan
item.description: Former Tyrone manager Feargal Logan feels the Red Hands "can beat Kerry" when they meet in Saturday's All-Ireland Senior Football Championship semi-final at Croke Park [18:15 BST].

item.title: Is Northern Ireland set for a heatwave?
item.description: Temperatures must reach at least 25C on three consecutive days for Northern Ireland to meet official heatwave status.

item.title: Who is Northern Ireland's new school behaviour tsar?
item.description: The former teacher has carried out reviews of behaviour in schools in England and critics say he has a hardline approach.

item.title: Masked youths attack bus twice
item.description: The bus was attacked at two locations in the Waterside area of Londonderry in the early hours of Sunday.

item.title: Skies lit up by 'epic' lightning show of 9,000 strikes
item.description: Almost 9,000 lightning strikes were recorded on Thursday night - mostly across Counties Londonderry and Donegal.

item.title: Bloody Sunday victim's brother criticises 'death threat' sentence
item.description: Dean Portis, 42, from Belfast, sent social media posts to three brothers of William McKinney during the Soldier F trial.

item.title: Everything you need to know about the Foyle Maritime Festival
item.description: The four-day celebration of Derry's maritime history will see the banks of the Foyle transformed into a mix of spectacle, colour and history.

item.title: How is Derry City's new pitch 'World Cup standard'?
item.description: Ahead of Friday's first game back at the Brandywell, BBC Sport learns how a giant sewing machine has helped put Derry City's new hybrid pitch together.

item.title: Magee student target date 'under review', MLAs told
item.description: The campus currently has about 6,500 students, with a target of reaching 10,000 students by 2032.

item.title: Supermarket given green light after five-year planning process
item.description: The proposed store near Crescent Link retail park in Londonderry was first submitted back in 2021.

item.title: Theatre to remain shut until 2027 amid repairs after water damage
item.description: The theatre closed in September last year and was previously expected to remain shut for about six months.

item.title: Two men injured in 'racially-motivated' attack
item.description: The two men were assaulted during an altercation and subjected to racist abuse, police say.

item.title: Man charged with assaulting ex-mayor Lilian Seenoi-Barr
item.description: Stephen Nigel Thompson is also accused of possessing material likely to cause fear and distress and engaging in behaviour likely to stir up hatred.

item.title: Animation company first in NI to have series on Apple TV
item.description: My Brother the Minotaur made its debut on Apple TV in April.

item.title: Hundreds of nurses given safety alarms after racist violence
item.description: The alarms were distributed to international nurses based at Altnagelvin Hospital in Londonderry.

item.title: Three Irish people die in crash in Spain
item.description: The crash happened near the popular tourist destination of Málaga at about 02:30 local time.

item.title: Ryanair says it will reluctantly not charge parents to sit next to children
item.description: The airline had typically charged adults a fee of £8 each way to sit with their young children.

item.title: Man charged with murder of woman found dead
item.description: Ali Sohrabi had been remanded in custody last month after he was charged with the murder of Masumeh Manojan.

item.title: Decriminalise all personal use drug possession, Irish committee says
item.description: The Oireachtas Committee on Drugs published its final report on Wednesday after spending months interviewing witnesses.

item.title: Irish government to contribute £197m to cross-border rail services
item.description: The money will come from the last allocation from the Irish government's Shared Island Fund.

item.title: Teenager charged over death of mother and child in crash
item.description: The charges included dangerous driving causing the death of 27-year-old Natalie McLaughlin and her daughter, six-year-old Ella.

item.title: Tributes paid to 'popular' teenager killed in Donegal rally crash
item.description: Tadhg Callaghan Carter, 15, was watching the Donegal International Rally when he was struck by a vehicle.

item.title: Boy dies after being hit by a car in County Mayo
item.description: The incident happened on the N59 at Eskeragh, near Ballina, County Mayo, on Sunday morning.

item.title: Why do people celebrate Bloomsday?
item.description: On one day every June, the streets of Dublin are transformed to a bygone era as James Joyce fans celebrate his work.

item.title: O'Leary extends Ryanair contract in deal that could net him over £130m
item.description: The Ryanair boss extends his contract to 2032, in a deal featuring a bonus scheme that could earn him more than €150m (£130m).

item.title: Irish PM warns about deep-fakes after financial scam video
item.description: Micheál Martin says the video was "clearly very false material" and serves as a reminder to be "vigilant on social media".

item.title: In Case You Missed It
item.description: You may know them as a supermarket chain but in Northern Ireland Lidl is opening a pub.
